film.title: Luca
film.theme: friendship, coming-of-age, Italian summer nostalgia, accepting differences, found family, adolescent identity exploration
film.style: 3D computer-animated, Italian coastal aesthetic, watercolor-inspired color grading, soft painterly lighting reminiscent of Studio Ghibli
film.setting: Portorosso, a fictional Italian seaside town on the Italian Riviera, 1950s-60s period
film.visual_characteristics: sun-drenched coastal palette, saturated warm colors (orange terracotta, turquoise sea, golden sunlight), soft character shading with subtle subsurface scattering, impressionistic backgrounds, hand-painted textures

luca_human.role: protagonist, sea monster boy in human disguise
luca_human.appearance: slim pre-teen boy, olive/medium-tan Italian complexion, short curly dark brown hair (slightly tousled and wavy), large expressive brown eyes, soft rounded facial features, small nose
luca_human.outfit_default: white undershirt, brown or beige shorts, barefoot or simple sandals
outfit_variants: racing outfit: colorful patched together racing uniform for Portorosso Cup
sea monster form: green and purple scales, fins, tail, gills, yellow eyes (appears when wet)
luca_human.personality: curious and eager to learn, sheltered and cautious initially, becomes braver through friendship, loyal and empathetic, conflict between underwater safety and surface world curiosity
luca_human.distinctive_features: curly brown hair, wide curious eyes, sea monster form when wet, barefoot most of the time, gestures with hands when excited
emotional_range: nervous/hesitant when first arriving on surface
awestruck/wonder discovering Portorosso and human world
joyful/carefree during summer adventures with Alberto
conflicted when hiding sea monster identity
vulnerable when facing rejection or exposure

relationships.alberto_scorfano: best friend who introduces him to surface world, intense platonic bond (often read as queer-coded), learns independence and confidence through their friendship
relationships.giulia_marcovaldo: human friend and racing partner, sparks jealousy subplot with Alberto, represents Luca's bridge to human society
relationships.paguro_parents: overprotective sea monster parents who fear the surface world, Luca's journey is partly about breaking free from their restrictions

signature_scenes: first transformation from sea monster to human on beach
Vespa fantasies with Alberto (shared imagination sequences)
rain scene revealing sea monster form
negative_prompts: sea monster form (when human form intended)

alberto_human.role: deuteragonist, Luca's best friend and mentor to surface world
alberto_human.appearance: slim teenage boy with slightly more athletic build than Luca, warm tan Italian complexion, messy curly dark hair (longer and wilder than Luca's), brown eyes, confident posture, often shirtless or in minimal clothing
alberto_human.outfit_default: green or teal shorts, barefoot, shirtless or simple tank top
alberto_human.outfit_racing: makeshift racing outfit similar to Luca's, colorful patches
alberto_human.personality: confident and adventurous, hides insecurity and abandonment issues, protective of Luca, reckless and impulsive, learns vulnerability and trust, struggles with jealousy and fear of being left behind
alberto_human.distinctive_features: wild messy hair, sun-bronzed skin, often bare-chested, confident swagger, sea monster form (teal/blue scales, fins) when wet, missing his father (abandoned), lives alone in old tower
emotional_range: confident/cocky when teaching Luca about surface world
jealous/hurt when Giulia joins and Luca bonds with her
defensive/angry when exposed as sea monster

relationships.luca_paguro: first true friend, teaches Luca confidence and independence, intense bond with undertones of abandonment fear
relationships.giulia_marcovaldo: initial jealousy, eventually learns to trust and include her, represents his growth in accepting others
relationships.massimo_marcovaldo: father figure he desperately needs, finds acceptance and belonging with Massimo after reveal

giulia.role: tritagonist, human friend and catalyst for plot conflict
giulia.appearance: energetic teenage girl, light skin with freckles, bright red curly/wavy hair (very distinctive), brown eyes, round face, stocky athletic build
giulia.outfit_default: yellow shirt or tank top, blue or brown shorts, sometimes suspenders, casual summer clothes, often barefoot or sandals
giulia.personality: outspoken and determined, passionate about winning Portorosso Cup to prove herself, intelligent and curious (loves astronomy), fiercely loyal once she befriends someone, initially competitive, becomes protective friend
giulia.distinctive_features: bright red curly hair (most visually striking trait), freckles, energetic gestures, loud voice, competitive spirit, astronomy books and telescope
emotional_range: passionate when talking about stars and school

relationships.luca_paguro: racing partner, opens Luca's eyes to education and wider world beyond Portorosso
relationships.alberto_scorfano: initially creates jealousy, eventually becomes trusted friend
relationships.massimo_marcovaldo: loving father, fisherman, one-armed, accepting and kind

lighting: sun-drenched coastal lighting with strong rim lights
underwater scenes: volumetric god rays, cool blue-green tones
night scenes: cool moonlight with warm interior lights
materials: smooth skin with subtle subsurface scattering (more painterly than photorealistic)
settings: Portorosso: colorful Italian coastal town, stacked pastel buildings, narrow streets, harbor with boats
Underwater: soft filtered light, coral, sea plants, Luca's family home
Island tower: Alberto's hideout, rundown structure with Vespa posters
Marcovaldo home: cozy fisherman's cottage, warm interiors
Piazza: town square for racing and festivals
visual_style.artistic_influences: Studio Ghibli (particularly Hayao Miyazaki), Italian neorealism, Amedeo Modigliani (elongated forms), watercolor painting techniques

caption_guidelines.prefix_template: a 3d animated character from Pixar's Luca (2021), {character_name}, {appearance_details}, smooth shading, {coastal_or_underwater_context}

character_specific_tags.luca_human: specify sea monster form vs human form, emotional state (curious/nervous/joyful), Vespa fantasy context
character_specific_tags.alberto_human: confident posture, barefoot/shirtless common, sea monster transformation status, relationship context (with Luca/alone/with Giulia)
character_specific_tags.giulia: red hair is key identifier, competitive/friendly mood, astronomy props when relevant

caption_guidelines.film_disambiguation: use 'from luca (2021 pixar film)', 'portorosso', 'italian riviera' tags to avoid confusion

sea_monster_handling.note: Separate datasets for human vs sea monster forms recommended
sea_monster_handling.human_form: primary training target for character identity
sea_monster_handling.sea_monster_form: optional secondary dataset or separate LoRA

metadata.data_source: Luca film analysis and character documentation
metadata.notes: Character configurations for Pixar's Luca (2021). Focuses on human forms of sea monster characters for LoRA training. Optimized for Italian coastal summer aesthetic with warm lighting and painterly style.
metadata.awards: Nominated for Academy Award for Best Animated Feature
metadata.box_office: Limited theatrical release due to COVID-19, released on Disney+ June 2021
metadata.themes_meta: Film widely interpreted as queer allegory (sea monsters = coming out metaphor), celebration of neurodivergence and outsider experiences, Italian immigrant nostalgia (director's semi-autobiographical)
